title: dianthus costae subsp. turolensis
intro: Dianthus costae subsp. turolensis, also known as Turolense Pink or Turolense Carnation, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Dianthus costae subsp. turolensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
